items.title: Revenue Operations Control Tower: operating discovery
items.name: Revenue Operations Control Tower: operating discovery
items.summary: Discovery work is testing whether revenue operations control tower can move the linked customer and business KPIs while addressing automation, support deflection, and better operating transparency without creating shadow workflows.
items.opportunity: Clarify whether this bet is strong enough to scale in the next planning cycle, or whether the organization should narrow scope and protect focus.

linkedInitiatives.description: Revenue Operations Control Tower Initiative is the quarter's focused investment to move Net New Revenue Attainment and Annual Platform Value Realized, anchored in automation, support deflection, and better operating transparency without creating shadow workflows and led through Unified revenue data model. The quarter concentrates on workflow automation, AI-assisted support deflection, and stronger operating transparency so the work reads like a realistic OKR-driven investment set for a company with roughly 250 product and technology people.

assignments.how: Leads the discovery framing, evidence synthesis, and recommendation on how far this initiative should scale.

assignments.teamName: Revenue Operations Platform - Integration Adapters Migration
assignments.how: Adds workflow, commercial, and implementation evidence needed to close the main delivery risks.

assignments.teamName: Revenue Operations Platform - Propensity Deal Scoring
assignments.how: Adds workflow, commercial, and implementation evidence needed to close the main delivery risks.

assignments.teamName: Enterprise Data Office - Enterprise Metric Layer
assignments.how: Adds workflow, commercial, and implementation evidence needed to close the main delivery risks.

riskFocus.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Customers will engage more consistently if revenue operations control tower measurably improves net new revenue attainment.
evidenceToCollect: Whether target users describe net new revenue attainment as a top-priority pain point.
Whether the proposed workflow changes are strong enough to change behavior, not just preference.

riskFocus.question: Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The investment is worth scaling when it improves annual platform value realized without creating disproportionate operating cost or support load.
evidenceToCollect: Which economic or operating constraints cap the upside.
What minimum KPI movement would justify continued investment.

riskFocus.question: Can customers figure out how to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Users can complete the redesigned workflow with less guidance and less back-and-forth than the current path.
evidenceToCollect: Where users hesitate in the new flow.
Which messages, states, or transitions remain unclear.

riskFocus.question: Can we build it within given resources and constraints?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The teams can deliver the smallest useful slice inside current architecture and staffing constraints without creating a long tail of one-off work.
evidenceToCollect: What can be standardized versus localized or custom.

plannedActivities: Replay the highest-volume internal service flows and isolate steps still handled manually.
Interview support, operations, and finance users about exception handling and policy workarounds.
Test where AI-assisted workflow support reduces handling time versus where it adds review burden.
items.decisionToMake: Decide which workflows should become the next default automation path and which should remain high-touch because of policy or judgment needs.

outcome.summary: Discovery closed enough uncertainty to make a concrete sequencing and investment decision for the following quarter.

items.title: Demand Engine Control Tower: operating discovery
items.name: Demand Engine Control Tower: operating discovery
items.summary: Discovery work is testing whether demand engine control tower can move the linked customer and business KPIs while addressing automation, support deflection, and better operating transparency without creating shadow workflows.
items.opportunity: Clarify whether this bet is strong enough to scale in the next planning cycle, or whether the organization should narrow scope and protect focus.

linkedInitiatives.description: Demand Engine Control Tower exists to Reduce handoff and attribution noise in the demand engine so marketing can create more qualified pipeline without scaling manual ops work. The quarter concentrates on workflow automation, AI-assisted support deflection, and stronger operating transparency so the work reads like a realistic OKR-driven investment set for a company with roughly 250 product and technology people.
